title: Admin Product Management Flow
interaction_flow: Admin → AddProductSrv: POST/GET /AddProductSrv (multipart: name, type, info, price, quantity, image)
AddProductSrv → HttpSession: getAttribute('usertype') — guard: must equal 'admin'
AddProductSrv → HttpSession: getAttribute('username'), getAttribute('password') — guard: not null
AddProductSrv → Part: getInputStream() — extract image InputStream
AddProductSrv → ProductServiceImpl: addProduct(prodName, prodType, prodInfo, prodPrice, prodQuantity, prodImage)
ProductServiceImpl → IDUtil: generateId() — create unique prodId
ProductServiceImpl → ProductBean: new ProductBean(prodId, name, type, info, price, qty, image)
ProductServiceImpl → DBUtil: provideConnection() — singleton JDBC connection
Admin → UpdateProductSrv: POST/GET /UpdateProductSrv (pid, name, type, info, price, quantity)
UpdateProductSrv → HttpSession: getAttribute('usertype') — guard: must equal 'admin'
UpdateProductSrv → ProductBean: new ProductBean(); setProdId, setProdName, setProdType, setProdInfo, setProdPrice, setProdQuantity
ProductServiceImpl → ProductServiceImpl: getProductQuantity(prevProductId) — fetch current qty from DB
ProductServiceImpl → Database: UPDATE product SET pname,ptype,pinfo,pprice,pquantity WHERE pid=?
[if update success AND new quantity > prev quantity]
ProductServiceImpl → DemandServiceImpl: haveDemanded(prevProductId) — get demand list
ProductServiceImpl → UserServiceImpl: getFName(demand.userName) — for each demand
ProductServiceImpl → MailMessage: productAvailableNow(email, firstName, prodName, prodId)
ProductServiceImpl → DemandServiceImpl: removeProduct(userName, prodId) — clear demand entry
RemoveProductSrv → HttpSession: getAttribute('usertype') — guard: must equal 'admin'
ProductServiceImpl → Database: DELETE FROM product WHERE pid=?
[if delete success] ProductServiceImpl → Database: DELETE FROM usercart WHERE prodid=?  (cascade cart cleanup)
ProductServiceImpl → Database: SELECT image FROM product WHERE pid=?
[if image == null] ShowImage → FileSystem: read images/noimage.jpg via ImageIO
key_relationships: UpdateProductSrv extends HttpServlet, depends_on ProductServiceImpl, depends_on ProductBean
ProductServiceImpl depends_on DBUtil (static utility, singleton connection)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on DemandServiceImpl (demand notifications on restock)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on UserServiceImpl (fetch user first name for email)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on MailMessage (send restock notification emails)
DBUtil manages singleton java.sql.Connection via DriverManager; reads application.properties

db_operations.removeProduct: DELETE FROM product WHERE pid=? + DELETE FROM usercart WHERE prodid=?
db_operations.updateProductWithoutImage: UPDATE product SET pname,ptype,pinfo,pprice,pquantity WHERE pid=?
db_operations.getImage: SELECT image FROM product WHERE pid=?
db_operations.getProductQuantity: SELECT pquantity FROM product WHERE pid=?

important_notes: All three admin servlets guard with session check: usertype=='admin' else redirect to login.jsp
addProduct has two overloads: one takes raw params (generates ID via IDUtil), the other takes ProductBean directly
updateProductWithoutImage triggers restock email notifications via MailMessage if quantity increases
removeProduct cascades: also deletes matching rows from usercart table
ShowImage falls back to noimage.jpg (via ImageIO) when DB returns null bytes
DBUtil.closeConnection(Connection) is intentionally no-op (connection reuse pattern — code is commented out)
